mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate over the prior quarter lowers discount rates and relieves financial conditions, so S&P 500 returns are positive over the next 20 trading days when policy is easing. The signal is falsified if rate cuts do not coincide with better short-horizon equity performance.
failure_condition: If 20-day forward SPX returns are not higher after 3-month declines in FEDFUNDS, or triggered cases produce negative median returns over any 2-year rolling window, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is above 4.5% and CPIAUCSL is still elevated, the market prices tighter liquidity and slower future growth, so SPX forward returns over the next 20 trading days are negative. The effect should disappear once inflation falls and policy is no longer restrictive.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 4.5% and CPIAUCSL rises above its 6-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations do not show negative median 20-day SPX returns, or if the sign is positive in more than 60% of triggered cases across any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above a recent trend signals labor-market deterioration and lower future household income, which should reduce equity prices over the next 60 trading days. The mechanism is testable because the return response should strengthen after unemployment breaks above trend.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if unemployment breakouts are followed by positive 60-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the median 60-day return after the trigger is not below the unconditional median.

mechanism_narrative: A broad monthly increase in RSAFS indicates stronger consumer demand and revenue growth, which should support SPX returns over the next 20 trading days. The signal fails if sales growth does not translate into better near-term equity performance.
failure_condition: Reject if positive changes in RSAFS are not associated with higher 20-day SPX returns, or if the rolling 2-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When INDPRO rises while CPIAUCSL is flat or falling, firms are expanding real output without an inflation penalty, which should lift equity returns over the next 60 trading days. The signal is falsified if production gains occur alongside weak or negative SPX performance.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises above its 3-month average while CPIAUCSL does not accelerate
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition does not produce above-baseline 60-day SPX returns, or if the sign of the effect is negative in more than half of triggered observations.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in HOUST reflects tightening mortgage sensitivity and weakening housing demand, which should lead SPX returns lower over the next 20 trading days. The hypothesis is falsified if housing starts contractions do not precede market weakness.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls by more than 10% from its 3-month average
failure_condition: Reject if large drops in HOUST are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in most cases, or if median returns after the trigger are not significantly below zero.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in PAYEMS and fall in UNRATE signals broad labor-market improvement, supporting consumption and earnings growth that should lift SPX returns over the next 20 trading days. This is a joint signal because payroll gains without falling unemployment are not sufficient.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS rises above its 3-month trend and UNRATE falls by at least 0.1 percentage points
failure_condition: Reject if the combined labor-improvement condition does not predict positive 20-day SPX returns, or if either proxy alone explains the effect better than the interaction.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained acceleration in GDPC1 indicates real output growth that should support equity valuations over the following quarter, especially after prior weak growth. The signal is falsified if stronger GDP does not translate into positive 60-day SPX returns.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 rises above its prior-quarter growth rate
failure_condition: Reject if GDP acceleration is not followed by positive 60-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the median return after the trigger is indistinguishable from unconditional performance.
